steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student must both explain Kepler's first law (orbits are elliptical) and provide a concrete example that demonstrates the elliptical principle — not orbital period, distance, or anything else.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Umbriel's orbit of Uranus is elliptical, demonstrating Kepler's first law of planetary motion, which describes the elliptical orbits of planets but applies to moons as well.: Explains that the law applies to natural satellites and uses Umbriel's elliptical orbit to demonstrate the elliptical principle directly. ✓
  • Choice B — Kepler's first law of planetary motion states that the orbit of a planet around the Sun is an ellipse; for example, planetary satellites orbit their planets in an elliptical fashion.: States a general point about planetary satellites rather than using the specific Umbriel example from the notes. ✗
  • Choice C — Kepler's first law of planetary motion, which describes the orbits of Uranus and other planets in the solar system, states that the object being orbited is one of the ellipse's foci.: States the law but gives nothing concrete to illustrate it. ✗
  • Choice D — Uranus's moon Umbriel completes an orbit in 4.14 Earth days on average, a clear example of Kepler's first law of planetary motion, which describes the elliptical orbit of planets.: Orbital period is unrelated to orbital shape — the law is about ellipses, not timing. ✗
A is correct.
